hypothesis: If a ∆-learning approach is used to predict the correction between computationally inexpensive GFN1-xTB semi-empirical band gaps and high-level DFT (PBE or HSE06) band gaps using XGBoost with structural descriptors, then the resulting model will achieve higher accuracy in predicting DFT-quality band gaps for metal-organic frameworks than either direct machine learning models trained on DFT band gaps alone or deep learning benchmarks, because learning the systematic, structured error between methods is an easier regression target than learning absolute band gap values from scratch.
